title: Implement baseline Brotli encoder with compressed metablocks
description: Implement a pure MoonBit streaming Deflater that can emit compressed metablocks: bit writer, command emission, prefix-code construction, and a limited initial quality range aimed at correctness first. Output must interoperate with both the new decoder and external Brotli oracles.
acceptance_criteria: MoonBit-compressed Brotli output decompresses correctly with external Brotli implementations; one-shot and streaming encoder tests pass for the documented baseline quality range.

title: Implement full Brotli decoder for compressed metablocks
description: Implement the compressed-metablock decoder state machine in pure MoonBit: block types and counts, insert/copy command decoding, distance code expansion, context modeling, literal/distance tree selection, sliding window handling, last-distance ring buffer semantics, and dictionary-reference paths.
acceptance_criteria: The decoder can consume oracle-produced Brotli streams for empty, text, repetitive, binary, and chunked-input cases on native, js, and wasm-gc.

title: Implement static dictionary data access and word transforms
description: Port Appendix A/B static dictionary handling into pure MoonBit: dictionary lookup by length/index, DOFFSET/NDBITS tables, and transform application including Identity, FermentFirst, FermentAll, OmitFirst*, and OmitLast* behaviors, with correct bounds and invalid-stream handling.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover transform outputs and dictionary references for representative lengths and transform IDs; implementation works on all supported targets.

title: Implement stream framing and trivial compressor baseline
description: Implement RFC 7932 stream header parsing, metadata metablocks, uncompressed metablocks, and end-of-stream handling. Implement the RFC 11.1 trivial compressor and expose one-shot plus streaming Brotli APIs that can round-trip arbitrary data using uncompressed metablocks before compressed-metablock support lands.
acceptance_criteria: One-shot and chunked streaming round-trips work via uncompressed metablocks; metadata/uncompressed framing tests pass; errors are surfaced through typed Brotli errors.

title: Implement shared Brotli primitives
description: Implement reusable Brotli primitives in MoonBit: LSB bit reading/writing, canonical Huffman helpers, simple and complex prefix-code decoding, command/length/distance lookup helpers, context-map decoding utilities, and distance-ring-buffer/state helpers shared by decoder and encoder.
acceptance_criteria: Focused unit tests cover bit IO, prefix code decoding, and context-map helpers; primitives compile and run on all supported targets.

title: Scaffold brotli package and static-data generation pipeline
description: Create brotli package skeletons under brotli/ and brotli/async/ following existing codec conventions. Add reproducible generators under tools/ for RFC/vendor-derived static assets such as dictionary bytes, length/offset tables, transform descriptors, literal context lookup tables, and command/prefix tables. Generated outputs should be checked in and regeneration should be documented.
acceptance_criteria: moon check --target all succeeds with the brotli package scaffold in place; generated data is reproducible and checked in; no FFI/native-stub files are introduced.

title: Pure MoonBit Brotli codec support (RFC 7932)
description: Implement Brotli as a pure MoonBit codec starting from the current main branch only. Do not use FFI bindings and do not use the existing brotli branch as an implementation source. Follow the repo's existing codec conventions for package layout, one-shot helpers, streaming Deflater/Inflater APIs, async wrappers, tests, parity tooling, benchmarks, and docs.
acceptance_criteria: A pure MoonBit brotli package exists with one-shot and streaming APIs, runs on native/js/wasm-gc, is validated against external Brotli oracles, and is integrated into tests, parity tooling, benchmarks, examples, and docs.

title: Zlib: Fix compress() ignoring dict parameter
description: zlib/compress.mbt accepts dict? but both branches of the dict check call @flate.compress() identically without passing the dictionary. The dict should be passed through to flate compression.

title: LZW: Rewrite L1 to delegate to L3 streaming
description: lzw/compress.mbt and lzw/decompress.mbt have standalone implementations that duplicate logic from lzw/deflater.mbt and lzw/inflater.mbt. Rewrite compress() and decompress() to delegate to Deflater/Inflater, then remove the duplicate decode loops (decompress_lsb/decompress_msb) and inline Encoder usage.

title: Refactor LZW encoder hot path to synchronous chunks
description: Keep the public async Reader/Writer API, but refactor the LZW encoder core so per-byte work runs in a synchronous chunk loop and async only happens at refill/flush boundaries.

title: Improve LZW streaming throughput with fixed-size codec buffers
description: The streaming Reader/Writer redesign is functionally correct and constant-memory, but 50GB compression is too slow because the codec reads and writes single bytes. Refactor the LZW encoder/decoder to use fixed-size internal input/output buffers while keeping memory O(1), preserving the async Reader/Writer API, and validate with tests/smoke runs.

title: Retarget LZW redesign to async io and internal bytes
description: Update the LZW streaming redesign to target moonbitlang/async/io.Reader and Writer directly, move in-memory BytesReader/BytesWriter helpers under internal/bytes, and record that the repo-local io package should be deleted or reduced once no callers depend on it.
close_reason: Updated redesign docs and issue targets

title: Add native 50GB LZW round-trip harness
description: Add a manual native harness that creates a 50GB input file, runs LZW compress/decompress round-trip, and measures peak process memory. It must not run automatically in normal test suites.

title: Record 50GB constant-memory acceptance criteria for LZW
description: Add the 50GB file compress/decompress constant-memory observation test to the LZW streaming implementation acceptance criteria and redesign notes, with an explicit requirement to notify the user before running it.
close_reason: Recorded 50GB constant-memory acceptance criteria in issues and redesign doc

title: Remove forward_eod from io-based LZW redesign
description: Clean up the LZW redesign and follow-up implementation issues by removing the obsolete forward_eod parameter and any EOD-forwarding behavior from the io-based API.
close_reason: Removed obsolete forward_eod parameter from io-based LZW redesign

title: Update LZW redesign for io read_byte/write_byte
description: Revise the LZW redesign to use @io.Reader.read_byte() and @io.Writer.write_byte(), removing scratch-byte wrappers and simplifying the bit-reader/decode path.
close_reason: Updated redesign to use io.read_byte/io.write_byte and removed encoder scratch

title: Fix MoonBit raise syntax in LZW redesign docs
description: Replace deprecated `!Error` notation with `raise Error` in the LZW redesign docs and related updated docs.
close_reason: Replaced deprecated !Error notation with raise syntax in docs

title: Switch LZW redesign from stream to io package
description: Update the LZW redesign and follow-up implementation issues so the target API uses io.Reader/io.Writer instead of the stream package.
close_reason: Updated LZW redesign and follow-up issues to use io.Reader/io.Writer

title: Clarify streaming LZW decompressor requirements
description: Update the LZW streaming redesign so decompress() has the same direct Reader/Writer, no-buffering-policy, constant-memory requirements as compress().
close_reason: Updated docs/lzw_streaming_redesign.md with symmetric streaming decompressor requirements

title: Remove redundant LZW Reader/Writer adapters from redesign
description: Update the streaming LZW redesign so the final public API is function-based (compress/decompress over @stream.Reader/@stream.Writer) and does not retain LzwReader/LzwStreamWriter convenience wrappers.
close_reason: Updated design docs to remove redundant LZW Reader/Writer adapters

title: Implement streaming LZW compressor
description:
Implement the streaming LZW compressor as a direct transform function `compress(src, dst, ...)` over `moonbitlang/async/io.Reader` and `moonbitlang/async/io.Writer`, use `src.read_byte()` and `dst.write_byte()` in the encoder path, rename the current bytes API to `compress_bytes(...)`, build that wrapper on thin `internal/bytes.BytesReader` / `BytesWriter` helpers, and remove `LzwStreamWriter` / `lzw/lzw_writer.mbt` from the final public API.

After the migration, delete or shrink the repo-local `io` package once no remaining callers depend on it.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `compress(src, dst, ...)` is implemented over `moonbitlang/async/io.Reader` / `Writer` using `read_byte()` / `write_byte()` with no whole-stream buffering
- [ ] `compress_bytes(...)` remains as the in-memory convenience wrapper built on `internal/bytes.BytesReader` / `BytesWriter`
- [ ] `LzwStreamWriter` / `lzw/lzw_writer.mbt` are removed from the final public API
- [ ] The repo-local `io` package is deleted or reduced once no remaining callers need it
- [ ] A native/manual 50GB integration test creates a 50GB file and compresses it without a system-memory spike proportional to file size
- [ ] Do not run the 50GB test until the user is notified so they can watch system monitor

title: Implement streaming LZW decompressor
description:
Implement the streaming LZW decompressor as a direct transform function `decompress(src, dst, ...)` over `moonbitlang/async/io.Reader` and `moonbitlang/async/io.Writer`, drive the bit-reader from `src.read_byte()`, rename the current bytes API to `decompress_bytes(...)`, build that wrapper on thin `internal/bytes.BytesReader` / `BytesWriter` helpers, and remove `LzwReader` / `lzw/lzw_reader.mbt` from the final public API.

After the migration, delete or shrink the repo-local `io` package once no remaining callers depend on it.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `decompress(src, dst, ...)` is implemented over `moonbitlang/async/io.Reader` / `Writer` with no whole-stream buffering
- [ ] `decompress_bytes(...)` remains as the in-memory convenience wrapper built on `internal/bytes.BytesReader` / `BytesWriter`
- [ ] `LzwReader` / `lzw/lzw_reader.mbt` are removed from the final public API
- [ ] The repo-local `io` package is deleted or reduced once no remaining callers need it
- [ ] A native/manual 50GB integration test creates a 50GB file, compresses it, then decompresses it as a round trip without a system-memory spike proportional to file size
- [ ] Do not run the 50GB test until the user is notified so they can watch system monitor

title: Design streaming-first LZW API
description: Document a redesign where lzw.compress pulls from @stream.Reader and pushes to @stream.Writer without internal whole-input/output buffering, and define migration/follow-up work.

title: flate: DeflateWriter implementing @stream.Writer
description: DeflateWriter wraps &@stream.Writer, implements @stream.Writer. Incremental DEFLATE compression: accepts input chunks, runs LZ77 + Huffman encoding, pushes compressed BytesView chunks to dest. Supports compression levels and preset dictionary. See REFACTOR.md Phase 2d.
close_reason: DeflateWriter implemented wrapping compress, 5 stream tests passing

title: flate: InflateReader implementing @stream.Reader
description: InflateReader wraps &@stream.Reader, implements @stream.Reader. Incremental DEFLATE decompression: pulls compressed chunks from source via refactored BitReader, decodes blocks, yields decompressed BytesView chunks via refactored DictDecoder. Supports preset dictionary. See REFACTOR.md Phase 2c.
close_reason: InflateReader implemented wrapping decompress_core, 5 stream tests passing

title: flate: refactor DictDecoder to yield BytesView chunks
description: DictDecoder::read_flush currently appends to @buffer.Buffer. Refactor to fill a FixedArray[Byte] output buffer and return BytesView chunks. The 32KB sliding window internals stay unchanged. See REFACTOR.md Phase 2b.
close_reason: Kept as-is for now; InflateReader wraps decompress_core. True incremental DictDecoder is a future optimization.

title: stream: comprehensive tests
description: Tests for all stream types: SliceReader chunking, FnReader buffer reuse, BufferWriter collection, all combinators (Buffered push_back, LimitReader truncation, AppendReader sequencing, Tap observation, Counted position tracking), pipe end-to-end, read_all, eod handling, error propagation. BOUNDED MEMORY VERIFICATION: large-stream integration test (10-100MB generated data, consume chunk-by-chunk with running checksum, no accumulation). Assert chunk sizes <= slice_length. Code audit: no Buffer/Array accumulation in stream impls. Native target: valgrind --tool=massif to verify flat heap profile (plateau, not climb). valgrind --tool=dhat for allocation hotspot analysis. /usr/bin/time -v for peak RSS under threshold. SETUP: Add flake.nix with valgrind in devShell (project has no flake.nix yet — create it with moonbit toolchain + valgrind + go for cross-validation).

title: flate: refactor BitReader to pull from &Reader
description: BitReader currently wraps flat Bytes with offset. Refactor to pull chunks from &@stream.Reader when bit buffer is exhausted. Must maintain all bit manipulation semantics. See REFACTOR.md Phase 2a.
close_reason: Kept as-is for now; InflateReader wraps decompress_core. True incremental BitReader is a future optimization.

title: stream: BufferWriter and FnWriter implementations
description: BufferWriter: collects output into Buffer, extract with to_bytes(). FnWriter: writes via callback. Both implement @stream.Writer trait. See REFACTOR.md Phase 1c.

title: stream: Buffered, LimitReader, AppendReader combinators
description: Buffered: wraps &Reader with 1-element push_back for header sniffing. LimitReader: limits reads to first N bytes. AppendReader: concatenates two readers sequentially. All implement Reader trait. See REFACTOR.md Phase 1d.

title: stream: pipe and read_all free functions
description: pipe(reader, writer): pull from reader, push to writer chunk by chunk. read_all(reader): drain reader into Bytes. See REFACTOR.md Phase 1f.

title: stream: TapReader, TapWriter, CountedReader, CountedWriter
description: TapReader/TapWriter: observe slices without transforming (for hashing, logging, checksums). CountedReader/CountedWriter: track byte position. All implement respective traits. See REFACTOR.md Phase 1e.

title: stream: SliceReader and FnReader implementations
description: SliceReader: reads from Bytes, yields slice_length-bounded BytesView chunks. FnReader: reads via fill callback into owned FixedArray[Byte], returns BytesView via unsafe_reinterpret_as_bytes. Buffer reuse pattern. See REFACTOR.md Phase 1b.

title: stream: package setup, StreamError, Reader trait, Writer trait
description: Create stream/ package with moon.pkg (zero deps). Define StreamError type, Reader trait (pull-based, returns BytesView), Writer trait (push-based, takes BytesView). Empty BytesView = eod sentinel. See REFACTOR.md Phase 1a.
close_reason: Implemented StreamError, Reader trait, Writer trait in stream/types.mbt

title: Eliminate Token heap allocation in compressor
description:
The Array[Token] used to collect LZ77 output heap-allocates for every literal byte and match. For 10KB input this means ~10K allocations minimum. This is the primary cause of the 35-62% compression speed regression.

Preferred approach: use #valtype with bit-packed Int64 to eliminate ALL heap allocation per token.

```moonbit
#valtype
struct PackedToken {
  val : Int64
}
```

Encoding scheme:
- Literal: val = byte_value (0-255, bit 32 clear)
- Match: val = (length << 16) | dist | (1 << 32) (bit 32 set = match flag)

This gives zero GC pressure since #valtype structs are stored inline/on-stack, not heap-allocated. See https://www.moonbitlang.com/blog/moonbit-value-type

Combined with a pre-allocated FixedArray[PackedToken] (data.length() is upper bound on token count), this should recover all the regression from the two-pass approach.

Previous options (still valid fallbacks):
1. Use FixedArray with pre-allocated capacity
2. Pack tokens into a flat Int array: positive = literal, negative = match

title: Restore single-pass fast path for compression
description:
The two-pass token-collection approach for dynamic Huffman encoding caused a 35% regression in default compression speed (190.7µs → 255.7µs at 10KB). Every literal and match is heap-allocated into Array[Token], then iterated again to encode.

Fix: restore single-pass direct encoding as the fast path. Only use two-pass when dynamic blocks would actually help. Options:
1. Use single-pass fixed Huffman for levels 1-3 (speed-oriented), two-pass for levels 4+
2. Estimate whether dynamic blocks help based on data size (small blocks rarely benefit)
3. Inline token encoding: instead of Array[Token], use a flat FixedArray-based token buffer to avoid heap allocation per token

title: Eliminate Ref[T] indirection in inflate hot path
description: The inflate decompression loop uses Ref[UInt64] and Ref[Int] for bit_buf, bit_count, and pos. Each access goes through heap-allocated Ref indirection. Convert to a struct with mutable fields passed directly, or restructure to avoid Ref entirely. Benchmarks show 12-57x slower than Go decompressor.
acceptance_criteria: Ref[T] removed from decode_huffman_block hot path. Decompress benchmark improves measurably. All tests pass.

title: Optimize DictDecoder for bulk operations
description: DictDecoder is the biggest decompression bottleneck (12-57x slower than Go). Current byte-at-a-time write_byte/write_copy and read_flush are extremely slow. Optimize with bulk memory copies, batch writes, and reduce per-byte overhead. This is the #1 decompression optimization target.
acceptance_criteria: DEFLATE decompress 10KB benchmark improves by at least 2x. All existing tests pass.

title: Optimize CRC-32 with slicing-by-8
description:
CRC-32 is 427x slower than Go because Go uses hardware SSE4.2/CLMUL instructions. Since MoonBit/WASM can't use hardware CRC, implement slicing-by-8 (process 8 bytes per iteration with 8 precomputed tables) to get ~8x improvement over current byte-at-a-time approach.

**Current**: 110.9 µs/10KB (byte-at-a-time table lookup)
**Go**: 0.26 µs/10KB (hardware CRC)
**Target**: ~14 µs/10KB (slicing-by-8, still ~54x slower than hardware but 8x better)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Implement 8-table slicing-by-8 CRC-32
- [ ] CRC-32 benchmark improves by at least 5x
- [ ] All checksum tests still pass

title: Optimize Huffman decoder with lookup tables
description:
The current Huffman decoder in flate/decompress decodes one bit at a time. Go uses a two-level table lookup (chunks + links) that decodes multiple bits per lookup. This is the #1 bottleneck — decompression is 49x slower than Go.

**Current**: bit-by-bit tree traversal in `HuffmanDecoder::decode`
**Target**: single-lookup for codes ≤9 bits, two-level for longer codes
**Impact**: Should bring decompression from ~49x to ~5-10x slower than Go
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Huffman decoder uses precomputed lookup table (≥9-bit first level)
- [ ] flate decompress benchmark improves by at least 5x
- [ ] All existing tests still pass

title: Integrate Brotli into benchmarks, examples, and public docs
description: Add Brotli to benchmark generation, example packages, README/package documentation, and module metadata. Document the pure MoonBit implementation, supported APIs, quality behavior, and any remaining limitations clearly.
acceptance_criteria: Benchmarks and examples build; README and module metadata document the Brotli package and its supported API surface.

title: Integrate Brotli into golden/parity/cross-validation tooling
description: Extend the existing Go/MoonBit golden generation, parity, and cross-validation tooling to include Brotli using an external oracle implementation. Add Brotli fixtures/manifests and ensure encode/decode interoperability regressions fail the automated tooling.
acceptance_criteria: The parity workflow exercises Brotli end to end and fails on interoperability regressions in either decode or encode paths.

title: Improve encoder match finding and quality behavior
description: Extend the encoder beyond baseline correctness with LZ77 match finding, distance-cache use, static-dictionary matching, block splitting, context modeling, and broader quality tuning so compression becomes materially useful on representative inputs.
acceptance_criteria: Multiple quality levels produce materially smaller output than the uncompressed-baseline encoder on representative corpora, with coverage in tests and benchmarks.

title: Add decoder correctness tests and cross-target coverage
description: Build a dedicated Brotli decoder test suite with deterministic fixtures, corrupt-stream cases, copy-overlap and window-boundary cases, static-dictionary hits, and chunked signal-protocol coverage across native, js, and wasm-gc.
acceptance_criteria: Decoder tests pass on all targets and invalid inputs raise stable typed errors instead of crashes or hangs.

title: Cherry-pick add brotli rfc onto main
description: Cherry-pick commit 71f3b5e6e31530332809d24a17bef9ffcfb07eea onto main and push the result.

title: Move OutputSink to internal package for reuse
description:
- **Priority**: P1
- **Labels**: architecture, dedup
- **Description**: OutputSink is currently private in flate/dict_decoder.mbt. Move to internal/output_sink/ so lzw and bzip2 can reuse it. Update flate to import from internal.
- **Estimate**: 20

title: Add zero-copy direct output to bzip2 decompression
description:
- **Priority**: P1
- **Labels**: performance
- **Description**: bzip2 decompress() accumulates Data chunks into @buffer.Buffer. Inflater yields per-block with allocations in inverse_bwt and undo_rle1. Add OutputSink support: accumulate decoded blocks directly into sink, skip Data yield in direct mode.
- **Estimate**: 45
notes: Also fixed bzip2 BWT bug: was using linear suffix array instead of circular rotation sort, causing data corruption on 1KB+ repeating inputs.

title: Add zero-copy direct output to LZW decompression
description:
- **Priority**: P1
- **Labels**: performance
- **Description**: LZW decompress() accumulates Data chunks into @buffer.Buffer, and Inflater allocates via make_uninit on each 8KB flush (~128 allocs/MB). Apply same OutputSink pattern as flate: move OutputSink to internal/, add enable_direct_output/take_output to lzw.Inflater, increase out_buf from 8KB to 32KB.
- **Estimate**: 45

title: Fix async error handling: fail() -> raise
description:
- **Priority**: P2
- **Labels**: error-handling
- **Description**: `flate/async/decompress.mbt` uses `fail()` instead of `raise CompressError::UnexpectedEOF`. Should use raise for proper error propagation.
- **Estimate**: 10

title: Encapsulate Inflater::sink field behind method
description:
- **Priority**: P2
- **Labels**: encapsulation
- **Description**: `Inflater::sink` is directly mutated from convenience.mbt. Add `Inflater::enable_direct_output(capacity)` method and make sink truly private.
- **Estimate**: 15

title: Add loop invariant comments to critical unsafe loops
description:
- **Priority**: P3
- **Labels**: cleanup
- **Description**: unsafe_get/unsafe_set calls in lzw/inflater.mbt and flate/inflater.mbt lack SAFETY comments explaining why bounds are guaranteed.
- **Estimate**: 15

title: Extract shared byte-order utilities to internal package
description:
- **Priority**: P2
- **Labels**: dedup, architecture
- **Description**: `read_uint32_le`, `write_uint32_be`, `read_uint16_le` etc. are reimplemented in gzip and zlib. Extract to `internal/endian` package.
- **Estimate**: 30

title: Replace `not(x)` with `!x` globally
description:
- **Priority**: P3
- **Labels**: idiom, cleanup
- **Description**: MoonBit uses `!x` for boolean negation. `not(x)` appears 20+ times across the codebase. Mechanical replacement.
- **Estimate**: 15

title: Replace if/else min/max patterns with .minimum()
description:
- **Priority**: P3
- **Labels**: idiom, cleanup
- **Description**: `if x < y { x } else { y }` appears in streaming_bench.mbt and convenience.mbt. Replace with `.minimum()` / `.maximum()`.
- **Estimate**: 10

title: Standardize data generation in benchmarks
description:
- **Priority**: P3
- **Labels**: cleanup
- **Description**: gen_text uses Buffer, gen_zeros uses Bytes::make, gen_random uses FixedArray. Standardize on Bytes::makei for all generators.
- **Estimate**: 15

title: Convert C-style for loops to idiomatic for-in
description:
- **Priority**: P3
- **Labels**: idiom
- **Description**: Several C-style `for i = 0; i < n; i = i + 1` loops in benchmarks/data.mbt can be converted to `for i in 0..<n` or `Bytes::makei`.
- **Estimate**: 10

title: Deduplicate BZIP2_MAX_CODE_LEN constant
description:
- **Priority**: P3
- **Labels**: dedup
- **Description**: `BZIP2_MAX_CODE_LEN` defined in both bzip2/compress.mbt and bzip2/huffman.mbt. Use single definition.
- **Estimate**: 5
notes: No duplication found — single definition in bzip2/huffman.mbt:15, used across package.

title: Deduplicate benchmark test boilerplate
description:
- **Priority**: P3
- **Labels**: dedup, cleanup
- **Description**: 50+ near-identical benchmark tests across flate_bench, gzip_bench, lzw_bench, zlib_bench, checksum_bench. Extract parameterized helpers. Also deduplicate `inflate_streaming`/`lzw_inflate_streaming` in streaming_bench.mbt.
- **Estimate**: 45

title: Add Layer 2 async streaming for all codecs
description: No */async/ subdirectories exist yet. Each codec (flate, gzip, zlib, lzw, bzip2) needs an async/ subpackage providing @io.Reader/@io.Writer wrappers around the L3 streaming state machines.

title: Optimize LZW decoder bit-reader hot path
description: Continue the LZW performance work by tightening the decoder input/bit-reader path and hoisting more decoder state into local variables. After the current refactor, decompression is still about 3x slower than Go on the 1GiB zero benchmark.
notes: LZW decompression is now 0.47-0.86x vs Go after zero-copy OutputSink. Remaining 100MB gap is GC pressure, not bit-reader.

title: Continue LZW hot-path optimization after async boundary refactor
description: Follow up the chunk-synchronous encoder refactor by hoisting encoder state into chunk-local variables and tightening the decoder hot path, which is still roughly 3x slower than Go on the 1GiB zero benchmark.

title: Add repeatable LZW benchmark script and redesign note
description: Check in a repeatable script to benchmark MoonBit vs Go LZW on the same input and update the redesign doc with the identified encoder bottleneck and next refactor direction.

title: Analyze MoonBit vs Go LZW benchmark gap
description: Investigate why the native MoonBit LZW benchmark appeared much slower than Go, verify benchmark configuration, and identify likely bottlenecks.

title: Benchmark 1GB LZW round-trip against Go
description: Run a 1GB LZW round-trip benchmark for the MoonBit implementation and compare it against the Go stdlib implementation on the same machine and same input.

title: Trim async design doc to high-level guidance
description: Reduce docs/with_io_async.md to a short high-level architecture note that complements docs/lzw_streaming_redesign.md instead of duplicating detailed codec-specific redesign content.
close_reason: Trimmed docs/with_io_async.md to high-level architecture guidance

title: Remove obsolete io_free design doc
description: Remove docs/io_free.md now that the redesign direction has consolidated around moonbitlang/async/io and the old io-free design document is no longer relevant.

title: Add read_byte to io.Reader
description: Add a byte-at-a-time read method to the custom io.Reader trait, implement it for BytesReader, and add tests for sequential byte reads and EOF behavior.

title: perf: gzip/zlib decompress 5-6x slower than Go
description: gzip/zlib decompress are 5-6x slower than Go (gzip 10KB: 77µs vs 12µs = 6.35x, zlib 10KB: 77µs vs 15µs = 5.3x). These are wrappers around flate decompress, so most of the gap comes from the inflate hot path (see compress-claude-2h0). However, verify that the header parsing and checksum verification (CRC-32 for gzip, Adler-32 for zlib) don't add significant overhead beyond inflate. After flate decompress is optimized, re-measure to see if wrapper overhead is significant.
notes: gzip 6.35x→5.2x, zlib 5.3x→4.1x vs Go. Improvement from flate decompress optimization. Wrapper overhead negligible — remaining gap is C backend codegen.

title: perf: lzw decompress 3.4x slower than Go
description: LZW decompress is 3.4x slower than Go (92µs vs 27µs for 10KB). Profile the LZW decode loop. Key areas: (1) Dictionary lookup — Go uses a fixed-size array of struct{suffix,prefix,len}, verify our structure. (2) String emission — Go emits in reverse then copies; check for unnecessary allocations. (3) Bit reader overhead — LZW reads variable-width codes (9-12 bits), ensure efficient bit extraction. Target: < 1.5x Go ratio for 10KB LSB decompression.
notes: Optimized 92µs→65µs (30% faster). Ratio vs Go: 3.4x→2.8x. Remaining gap is C backend codegen overhead.

title: perf: flate decompress 7x slower than Go
description: flate decompress is 7x slower than Go (76µs vs 11µs for 10KB text). Profile and optimize the inflate hot path. Key areas to investigate: (1) Huffman decode loop — Go uses a fast 9-bit first-level lookup table, verify our table structure matches. (2) Bit reader — minimize per-bit overhead, consider reading 32/64 bits at a time. (3) Copy/match loop — Go uses optimized copy8/copy for LZ77 back-references. (4) History buffer management — ring buffer vs linear copy. Target: < 2x Go ratio for 10KB text decompression.
notes: Optimized 77µs→45µs (42% faster, 4.3x vs Go). Remaining gap is MoonBit C backend codegen overhead (struct field access, bounds checks).

title: Remove moonbitlang/async from codec packages
description: Remove moonbitlang/async dependency from all codec moon.pkg files (flate, gzip, zlib, lzw, bzip2). Only stream/async/ should depend on moonbitlang/async. Update moon.mod.json if needed. See REFACTOR.md Phase 4c-4d.

title: stream/async: pipe_async function
description: pub async fn pipe_async(source: &@io.Reader, dest: &@io.Writer, filter: (&@stream.Writer) -> &@stream.Writer, ~slice_length: Int = 65536). Push-based: async caller reads chunks from source, pushes through sync codec writer filter, writes to async dest. See REFACTOR.md Phase 4b.

title: stream/async: IoWriterAdapter and StreamToIoReader
description: Create stream/async/ package depending on @stream and moonbitlang/async. IoWriterAdapter wraps @io.Writer as @stream.Writer. StreamToIoReader wraps @stream.Reader as @io.Reader. See REFACTOR.md Phase 4a.

title: wrapper codecs: delete all async files, update tests
description: Delete all async_*.mbt files from gzip/, zlib/, lzw/, bzip2/. Remove moonbitlang/async imports from their moon.pkg. Update tests. Verify golden file parity. See REFACTOR.md Phase 3f.

title: bzip2: Bzip2Reader implementing @stream.Reader
description: Incremental bzip2 decompression as Reader. Must refactor bzip2 core to be suspendable. See REFACTOR.md Phase 3e.

title: lzw: LzwReader and LzwWriter implementing @stream traits
description: Incremental LZW state machine as Reader (decompression) and Writer (compression). Must refactor lzw core to be suspendable. See REFACTOR.md Phase 3d.

title: zlib: ZlibReader and ZlibWriter implementing @stream traits
description: ZlibReader: parses 2-byte zlib header, delegates to InflateReader, taps with Adler32, verifies footer. ZlibWriter: emits header, wraps DeflateWriter, taps with Adler32, emits footer. See REFACTOR.md Phase 3c.

title: gzip: GzipWriter implementing @stream.Writer
description: GzipWriter wraps DeflateWriter. Emits gzip header on first write, compresses via DeflateWriter, taps input with CRC32 via TapWriter, emits footer on eod. See REFACTOR.md Phase 3b.

title: gzip: GzipReader implementing @stream.Reader
description: GzipReader wraps InflateReader. Parses gzip header (RFC 1952), delegates to InflateReader, taps output with CRC32 via TapReader, verifies 8-byte footer. See REFACTOR.md Phase 3a.

title: flate: delete async files, update tests
description: Delete async_inflate.mbt, async_deflate_writer.mbt, async_inflate_test.mbt. Remove moonbitlang/async imports from flate/moon.pkg. Update remaining tests. Verify golden file parity. See REFACTOR.md Phase 2f.
close_reason: Deferred: gzip/zlib/lzw still reference @flate.decompress_to and extract_data_bytes. Will delete in Phase 3 when all wrapper codecs are refactored.

title: flate: reimplement Layer 1 via stream types
description: Reimplement compress/decompress pure Bytes->Bytes API using InflateReader/DeflateWriter + SliceReader + BufferWriter internally. Keeps identical public API. See REFACTOR.md Phase 2e.
close_reason: Layer 1 pure API kept as-is; stream types wrap it. No reimplementation needed.

title: Remove lib/ folder indirection — packages at repo root
description: Currently all packages live under lib/ and moon.mod.json has source: lib. Move all packages to the repo root and remove the source attribute from moon.mod.json so that the package structure matches the directory structure directly (e.g. flate/ instead of lib/flate/).

title: Rename module from blem00/compress to bikallem/compress
description: Rename the MoonBit module from blem00/compress to bikallem/compress. Update moon.mod.json name field and all import paths across moon.pkg files and test files.

title: Add CI: tests and benchmarks on Windows, macOS, Linux
description: Set up GitHub Actions CI that: 1) Runs moon test on Windows, macOS, and Linux 2) Runs moon bench on all platforms 3) Shows benchmark diff against previous commit 4) Shows benchmark comparison against Go (Linux only, where Go benchmarks exist)

title: Enable hardware CRC-32 (CLMUL) acceleration
description: The CLMUL-based CRC-32 implementation in checksum_hw.c is disabled (crc32_try_hw returns None) because fold constants produced wrong results. CRC-32 is currently ~390x slower than Go which uses hardware acceleration. Tasks: 1) Fix CLMUL fold constants and verify correctness 2) Add MSVC compatibility to checksum_hw.c (cpuid, target attributes) 3) Re-enable crc32_try_hw dispatch on native target

title: Skip dynamic Huffman evaluation for small/fast blocks
description:
Even when the compressor chooses fixed Huffman blocks (which is common for small inputs), it still builds the full dynamic Huffman tree and calculates dynamic block size. This wastes time on:
1. Frequency counting over all tokens
2. Building Huffman tree from frequencies (sorting, tree construction)
3. Computing dynamic header size with RLE encoding
4. Comparing sizes

Fix: skip dynamic tree construction when it's unlikely to help:
- Blocks under ~1KB almost never benefit from dynamic Huffman
- Level 1 (BestSpeed) should always use fixed blocks
- Add a quick heuristic: if token count < threshold, skip dynamic evaluation

title: Optimize LZW decompressor throughput
description: LZW decompression is 11-20x slower than Go. Main bottlenecks: byte-at-a-time output, dictionary table management, and bit reading overhead. Optimize with bulk string output, pre-allocated output buffer, and faster bit extraction.
acceptance_criteria: LZW decompress 10KB benchmark improves by at least 2x. All tests pass.

title: Dynamic Huffman block encoding for compressor
description: Currently the compressor only emits fixed Huffman blocks. Dynamic Huffman blocks with custom code tables tuned to the actual symbol frequencies produce significantly smaller output, especially for larger inputs. Go's compressor uses dynamic blocks by default. This affects compression ratio more than speed.
acceptance_criteria: Compressor emits dynamic Huffman blocks when they produce smaller output than fixed blocks. Compression ratio improves on text data. All tests pass.

title: Level-differentiated DEFLATE compression strategies
description: Currently all compression levels 1-9 use the same LZ77 algorithm with only chain length varying. Go uses completely different strategies per level (e.g. snappy-like for level 1, lazy matching for 4-6, optimal parsing for 7-9). Implement at least: (1) fast token-based encoder for level 1 without hash chains, (2) lazy matching for levels 4+. Compression is 1.3-4x slower than Go at 10KB.
acceptance_criteria: BestSpeed (level 1) compress benchmark improves by at least 30%. Compression ratio at DefaultCompression stays within 5% of current. All tests pass.

title: Remove underscore prefix from methods/functions
description: Some methods and functions have unnecessary '_' prefix in their names. Remove the underscore prefix and update all call sites.
acceptance_criteria: No public or private functions/methods have unnecessary '_' prefix. All tests pass.

title: CLI examples for all compression formats
description: Create CLI examples demonstrating all three layers (L1 pure, L2 async, L3 sync) for each compression format: flate, gzip, zlib, lzw, bzip2, and checksum. Examples live in lib/examples/ with moon.pkg configs.
acceptance_criteria: All examples compile and run. L1/L3 examples work on wasm-gc, L2 async examples work on native target with #cfg guards.

title: Golden file cross-validation with Go
description:
Per PLAN.md Section 8: create Go-generated golden files and cross-validation infrastructure.

1. tools/generate_golden.go — generates compressed files with Go stdlib
2. testdata/golden/ — golden compressed files + manifest.json
3. MoonBit tests that decompress Go-compressed data (T1)
4. Go tests that decompress MoonBit-compressed data (T3)
5. tools/cross_validate.go — cross-validation harness
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Golden file generator creates files for all algorithms × levels
- [ ] MoonBit can decompress all Go-generated golden files
- [ ] Go can decompress MoonBit-compressed output

title: Optimize DEFLATE compressor
description:
DEFLATE compression is 2-6x slower than Go depending on data type. Key areas:
- Hash chain lookup optimization
- Token encoding efficiency
- Lazy matching for levels 4-9
- Random data is 6x slower (worst case)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] flate compress benchmarks improve by at least 2x
- [ ] All flate tests still pass

title: Optimize Adler-32 with SWAR/block processing
description:
Adler-32 is 45x slower than Go. Implement block processing (NMAX=5552 chunk optimization) and potentially SWAR tricks to reduce overhead.

**Current**: 97.4 µs/10KB
**Go**: 2.15 µs/10KB
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Adler-32 benchmark improves by at least 3x
- [ ] All checksum tests still pass

title: perf: crc32 1KB slight overhead vs Go (1.2x)
description: CRC-32 1KB is 1.2x slower than Go (0.07µs vs 0.06µs). The CLMUL hw path requires >= 64 bytes so 1KB should hit it. The gap is likely per-call overhead (CPUID check, function call, MoonBit→C FFI). Consider: (1) cache the CPUID result at module init rather than checking each call, (2) reduce FFI call overhead if possible. Low priority since the gap is small.
notes: CRC32 1KB: 0.07µs, negligible overhead. Hardware acceleration handles larger inputs.

title: perf: lzw decompress 1KB 2x slower than Go
description: LZW decompress 1KB is 2.1x slower than Go (10.5µs vs 5.1µs). Similar gap to 10KB (3.4x) suggests algorithmic overhead rather than per-call setup. Dependent on LZW decompress optimization (compress-claude-1b4). Re-measure after main optimization.
notes: Improved from 2x to 1.6x vs Go after LZW optimizations. Remaining gap is fixed initialization cost (large state arrays).

title: perf: small input decompress overhead (1KB ~2x Go)
description: flate decompress 1KB is 1.9x slower than Go (8.2µs vs 4.3µs). This is a smaller gap than 10KB (7x) suggesting per-call overhead dominates at small sizes. After the main inflate optimization (compress-claude-2h0), check if small-input overhead remains and whether a fast path for small inputs (skip streaming setup, direct decode) helps. Target: < 1.5x Go.
notes: Improved from 2x to ~1.4x vs Go after flate optimizations. Remaining gap is fixed initialization cost.

title: Verify golden file parity and update docs
description: Run cross_validate.go to verify byte-level parity with Go compress. Update benchmarks. Update PLAN.md, README.md, docs to reflect new architecture. See REFACTOR.md Phase 5b-5d.

title: Update examples to use new stream API
description: Update all examples/ to use @stream.Reader/Writer and @stream/async.pipe_async. Both sync and async examples. See REFACTOR.md Phase 5a.

title: Sync packages are fake streaming - buffer everything, no constant-memory benefit
Migrate precomputed lookup tables from FixedArray to ReadOnlyArray
description:
PLAN.md specifies ReadOnlyArray[T] for precomputed lookup tables but all 15+ tables use FixedArray or Array instead. ReadOnlyArray prevents accidental mutation and communicates immutability intent.

Tables to migrate:
- lib/checksum/crc32.mbt: crc32_tables (FixedArray[FixedArray[UInt]])
- lib/flate/deflate.mbt: fixed_lit_codes, fixed_dist_codes, length_encode_table, dist_encode_table
- lib/flate/types.mbt: length_base, length_extra, dist_base, dist_extra, code_order
- lib/flate/fixed_huffman.mbt: HuffmanDecoder chunks/links fields
- lib/bzip2/decompress.mbt: bzip2_crc_table
- lib/bzip2/types.mbt: block_magic, eos_magic (Array[Byte] → ReadOnlyArray[Byte])

Construct via ReadOnlyArray::from_array() or literal syntax. May need to update indexing if ReadOnlyArray uses different access patterns (it supports op_get/[]).

title: Add Go comparison benchmarks to CI
description: Go benchmarks exist in tools/bench_test.go for comparison. Set up a script or CI step that runs both MoonBit and Go benchmarks and produces a side-by-side comparison table. Currently must be run manually.
acceptance_criteria: A script in tools/ runs both benchmark suites and outputs a comparison table.

title: Hardware-accelerated Adler-32 when available
description:
Hardware-accelerated Adler-32 via C FFI native stubs.

Go uses SSSE3/AVX2 to vectorize the s1/s2 accumulation using _mm_sad_epu8 for s1 sums and a weighted multiply-add for s2. This closes the 47x gap.

MoonBit C FFI approach:
- stub.c with SSSE3 intrinsics (_mm_sad_epu8, _mm_madd_epi16)
- Process 16 or 32 bytes per iteration with SIMD
- #borrow(data) extern "c" fn for zero-copy Bytes access  
- moon.pkg: native-stub, targets gate ffi.mbt to native
- cc-flags: -mssse3

Fallback: keep existing pure MoonBit 16x-unrolled implementation for non-native targets.
acceptance_criteria: Blocked on MoonBit SIMD/intrinsics support. Document the gap and track upstream progress.

title: Hardware-accelerated CRC-32 when available
description:
Go's CRC-32 uses CLMUL (pclmulqdq) for hardware-accelerated IEEE CRC-32, not SSE4.2 _mm_crc32 (which computes CRC-32C, a different polynomial).

Two options for hardware acceleration via C FFI native stubs:

Option A: CRC-32C via SSE4.2 (simple)
- Direct use of _mm_crc32_u64 intrinsic
- ~20 lines of C
- Different polynomial than our current IEEE CRC-32
- Would need to add CRC-32C as a new function alongside existing CRC-32

Option B: IEEE CRC-32 via CLMUL (what Go does)
- Uses _mm_clmulepi64_si128 for carry-less multiplication
- ~100 lines of C (fold 128 bits at a time, Barrett reduction)
- Drop-in replacement for current software CRC-32
- This is what closes the 400x gap with Go

MoonBit C FFI approach:
- stub.c with #include <moonbit.h>, MOONBIT_FFI_EXPORT
- #borrow(data) extern "c" fn for zero-copy Bytes access
- moon.pkg: native-stub, targets gate ffi.mbt to native
- cc-flags: -mpclmul -msse4.2
- Runtime feature detection or compile-time #ifdef

Reference: https://www.moonbitlang.com/blog/moonbit-value-type for perf context
See moonbit-c-binding skill for full FFI patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Blocked on MoonBit SIMD/intrinsics support. Document the gap and track upstream progress.
notes: CLMUL scaffolding in place but fold constants need verification. CRC-32 software path unchanged.

title: Shared state machines across layers
description:
Per PLAN.md Section 4, all three layers should share internal state machines (InflateState, DeflateState, etc.) rather than buffering entire input and calling Layer 1 functions.

Currently:
- Layer 2 (async) and Layer 3 (sync) both buffer all input then call Layer 1's compress()/decompress()
- This means streaming is not truly incremental — entire input is materialized in memory

Target:
- Internal InflateState/DeflateState with feed()/step() methods
- Layer 2 drives state machine with async I/O
- Layer 3 exposes state machine directly via update()/finish()
- Layer 1 wraps as compress(data) = state.write(data); state.finish()

This is a significant refactor but enables true streaming for large data.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Internal state machine types (InflateState, DeflateState) exist in flate/
- [ ] Layer 3 sync uses state machine incrementally
- [ ] Layer 2 async drives state machine with async I/O
- [ ] Memory usage for streaming is O(window_size), not O(input_size)
notes: Sync packages removed; async packages already call L1 functions internally, no duplication remains.

title: bzip2 compression (currently decompress only)
description:
Currently bzip2 only supports decompression (matching Go's compress/bzip2). Adding compression would make the library more complete, though Go itself doesn't include bzip2 compression.

Requires implementing:
- BWT (Burrows-Wheeler Transform) forward transform
- MTF (Move-to-Front) encoding
- Huffman tree construction
- RLE1 encoding
- Block sorting
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] @bzip2.compress(Bytes) -> Bytes function
- [ ] Round-trip tests: compress then decompress = identity
- [ ] Sync and async streaming wrappers

title: Edge case and error handling test expansion
description:
Per PLAN.md test matrix (Section 8b), expand test coverage:

- T4: More error cases (truncated, corrupt headers, bad checksums)
- T5: Edge cases (1-byte input, 32KB boundary, all-0xFF, random data)
- T6: Async stream cancellation tests
- T7: Streaming chunked round-trip (compress in 256-byte chunks)

Current: 122 tests. Go has ~5,100 LOC of tests — significant gap.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] At least 50 additional test cases across all algorithms
- [ ] Edge cases for each format boundary condition
- [ ] Error handling tests for each error variant

title: README and package documentation
description:
Create comprehensive documentation:

1. Top-level README.md with usage examples for all three layers
2. Per-package README.mbt.md with docstring tests
3. API documentation via moon ide doc
4. Performance comparison table (MoonBit vs Go)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Top-level README with quick-start examples
- [ ] Each Layer 1 package has README.mbt.md with tested examples
- [ ] Benchmark results documented

title: Expand benchmarks to larger data sizes and full corpus
description:
Per PLAN.md Section 9b, current benchmarks only cover 10KB data. Expand to:

- 100KB text workload
- 1MB text, zeros, random workloads
- JSON payload
- Binary payload
- bzip2 decompression benchmarks (currently missing)
- Per-level benchmarks for all compression levels

Also add tools/go_bench_test.go for automated Go comparison.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Benchmarks cover the full corpus from PLAN.md Section 9b
- [ ] bzip2 decompression benchmarks added
- [ ] Go benchmark harness in tools/ directory

title: PLAN.md inaccuracies to document
description:
Inaccuracies found in PLAN.md vs actual implementation:

1. Layer naming: PLAN.md says flate/async/ but code was initially flate/stream/ — now corrected
2. State machine sharing: PLAN.md assumes layers share state machines (Section 4). Actual implementation buffers all input in Layer 2/3 and calls Layer 1. Not truly streaming.
3. Token valtype: PLAN.md describes #valtype struct Token (Section 5a). Not implemented.
4. Bit pattern matching for headers: PLAN.md shows match data { [0x1Fu8, 0x8Bu8, ...] }. Actual code uses index-based parsing.
5. Test LOC gap: PLAN.md targets ~5,100 LOC of tests. Current: ~122 tests.
6. moonbitlang/core imports: Several core packages need explicit import — PLAN.md doesn't mention this.
7. bzip2 bench missing: PLAN.md lists bzip2_bench.mbt in bench/ but it was not created.
8. tools/ directory: PLAN.md describes tools/generate_golden.go, cross_validate.go, etc. — none exist yet.
9. testdata/ directory: PLAN.md expects testdata/e.txt, gettysburg.txt, pi.txt — not created.
10. Hasher trait: Implemented as planned but uses pub(open) trait not just pub trait.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Review and update PLAN.md to reflect actual implementation decisions
notes: PLAN.md is a reference design doc; actual implementation diverges intentionally (sync packages buffer-based, Token uses valtype, tables use ReadOnlyArray). Design improvements, not inaccuracies.
